title: Coronary Artery Ligation in Mice
author: Richard Mcindoe
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wc4fayw
materials: Shave the left thoracic area of the mouse.

steps.title: Anesthetize the mouse and ensure depth...
steps.instruction: Anesthetize the mouse and ensure depth of anesthesia with a toe pinch.

steps.title: The mouse is intubated using a...
steps.instruction: The mouse is intubated using a 22-G angiocatheter sheath and placed on a rodent ventilator with the left chest up, secure feet to surgical area with tape.

steps.title: Prep the surgical field with 70%...
steps.instruction: Prep the surgical field with 70% isopropanol as well as Betadine solution and drape.

steps.title: A left side thoracotomy is performed...
steps.instruction: A left side thoracotomy is performed between the 3rd and 4th intercostal space and opened approximately 6-7 mm with a small retractor. Use caution to avoid injury to the lung.

steps.title: After making an opening the pericardium,...
steps.instruction: After making an opening the pericardium, the left anterior descending coronary artery is identified using a microscope. A 7-0 Proline suture is placed just under the descending coronary artery without entering the left ventricle. This can be tied permanently to ligate the LAD for an acute ischemia model or alternatively, the 7-0 proline suture is secured over a 3-4 mm piece of PE-90 tubing to ligate the LAD for 30 minutes, and then the suture is removed to facilitate reperfusion of the heart for an ischemia reperfusion model. The suture is placed 2 mm below the left atrium for a “large” infarction; 3 mm below the left atrium for a “medium” infarction; or 4 mm below the left atrium for a small infarction.

steps.title: Two separate 6-0 Proline sutures are...
steps.instruction: Two separate 6-0 Proline sutures are place to close the rib cage

steps.title: A 20g iv catheter sheath is...
steps.instruction: A 20g iv catheter sheath is left between the sutures to evacuate air after closure is complete.

steps.title: The dermis is sutured closed with...
steps.instruction: The dermis is sutured closed with 6-0 Proline, Tissue clips or glued with surgical glue

steps.title: Air is removed from thoracic cavity...
steps.instruction: Air is removed from thoracic cavity via the sheath and a 1-mL syringe. Gently pinching the skin around the sheath slowly draw back on the syringe while withdrawing the sheath.

steps.title: Animal is weaned off anesthesia and...
steps.instruction: Animal is weaned off anesthesia and extubated when spontaneous breathing resumes.

steps.title: Injection 0
steps.instruction: Injection 0.5 ml of 0.9% NaCl subcutaneously to each mouse to prevent dehydration.

steps.title: Post-procedure Analgesics
steps.instruction: Post-procedure Analgesics: Buprenorphine (0.05mg/kg) every 12 hours, for 72 hours post-op. Meloxicam (5mg/kg) every 24 hours, for 72 hours post-op Post-procedure Monitoring: Mice are monitored 2x daily for the first 72 hours after the surgery. Thereafter, mice are monitored at least 3x per week.
